NWS DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMED EF-1 TORNADO DAMAGE IN NORTHWEST BANNER COUNTY ON JUNE 22ND. TORNADO DEVELOPED 17 WNW HARRISBURG AT ABOUT 450 PM AND TRACKED ESE BEFORE DISSIPATING NEAR 10 NW HARRISBURG AT ABOUT 505 PM. ONE OUTBUILDING WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED WITH OTHERS DAMAGED. ROOF WAS REMOVED FROM A 3 STALL GARAGE. SEVERAL POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED AND CENTER PIVOTS FLIPPED OR TWISTED. MULTIPLE 6 TO 12 INCH DIAMETER TREES SNAPPED. PATH LENGTH 7.8 MILES. MAX WIDTH 100 YDS. MAX WIND GUSTS 100 MPH.
TORNADO VISIBLE ON THE GROUND BETWEEN 451 AND 456 PM MDT. REPORT FROM OFF DUTY SPC FORECASTER.

TORNADO PRODUCED DAMAGE AT A HOUSE 7 MILES NORTH AND 14 MILES WEST OF HARRISBURG...ABOUT 2.5 MILES EAST OF THE WYOMING STATE LINE. A HOUSE AT THAT LOCATION RECEIVED CONSIDERABLE DAMAGE AND 2 BARNS ON THE PROPERTY LOST ROOFS. SEVERAL TREES...POWER LINES AND POLES NEARBY WERE ALSO DOWNED.
CENTER PIVOT PARTIALLY OVERTURNED.
HAIL MOSTLY QUARTER SIZED WITH SOME TO SIZE OF TENNIS BALLS. HEAVY RAIN WITH VIS DOWN TO 1/8 MI. 2 WINDOWS DAMAGED. ONE CRACKED. ONE BROKEN OUT. BOTH ON NORTH SIDE OF HOUSE.
TORNADO VISIBLE ON THE GROUND BETWEEN 526 AND 532 PM MDT. TORNADO DISSIPATED SHORTLY THEREAFTER. REPORT FROM OFF DUTY SPC FORECASTER.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS SEVERELY DAMAGED A WHEAT CROP IN A 2 TO 3 MILE WIDE PATH...FROM 14 MILES NORTHWEST OF HARRISBURG TO 7 MILES EAST OF THAT LOCATION.
50 TO 60 MPH WIND GUSTS AND NICKEL SIZED HAIL AT THE INTERSECTION OF STONEGATE AND HIGHLAND ROADS.
